content:
The wood of the bench is cool tonight, but the keys have already taken on the heat of my hands. Nine o'clock. Tuesday. The air in Sheba’s smells of berbere, roasted coffee, and that specific San Francisco dampness that clings to the coats of the people walking in off Fillmore. 

I don't look at the door much anymore. I don't need to. I can hear the neighborhood changing by the way the street noise
